intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

body.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

draft.title: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

Building your first draft…

data.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer a friendly, encouraging tone with personality over clinical detachment in health tracking interfaces
facade.label: Warm and conversational
facade.acceptImplies: The dashboard should use warm language, celebratory feedback, and a supportive companion-like voice to motivate users through their experiments
facade.rejectImplies: The dashboard should adopt a clean, neutral, clinical tone focused on data presentation without personality or emotional engagement

evidence.hypothesis: Users prefer a friendly, encouraging tone with personality over clinical detachment in health tracking interfaces
evidence.implication: The dashboard should use warm language, celebratory feedback, and a supportive companion-like voice to motivate users through their experiments

facade.hypothesis: A health dashboard benefits from clean, spacious card layouts that reduce cognitive load and emphasize data clarity over visual decoration
facade.axisTargeted: Density vs. spaciousness in information architecture
facade.label: Minimalist cards
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers breathing room, white space, and singular focus per card — likely values calm and scanability
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer denser layouts, more information per view, or visual richness to convey progress and engagement

facade.hypothesis: Users building health experiments need persistent navigation + glanceable data summaries side-by-side, trading screen width for always-visible context
facade.axisTargeted: Layout density and navigation persistence — sidebar presence vs top tabs; card-based summary vs list-based drill-down
facade.label: Dense sidebar with mini-cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values at-a-glance status checks and quick experiment switching; favors compact layouts with persistent structural anchors
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers full-width content and tab-based navigation; may find sidebars constraining or cluttered for health data exploration

facade.hypothesis: Smooth, gradual animations between health metric views reduce cognitive load and create a sense of continuous progress rather than jarring jumps between data states
facade.axisTargeted: Motion and behavior—fluid vs snappy transitions
facade.label: Gentle fade transitions
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers ease-of-understanding through motion; likely values calm, continuous interaction patterns that emphasize gradual change and trend-following over instant state swaps
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer snappy, instant feedback; likely values quick scannability and minimal animation overhead, suggesting a dashboard design that prioritizes speed of data access over smooth visual continuity

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
axes.poleA: All at once or step by step?

axes.label: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking experience
Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking experience
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users building health dashboards face a fundamental choice: show change over time (line charts, trends) or current state (gauges, key numbers today). This tension shapes every information design decision downstream.
facade.axisTargeted: Temporal representation — do you prioritize historical narrative or present-moment clarity?
facade.label: Timeline vs snapshot
facade.acceptImplies: User leans toward trend visibility, comparative context, pattern recognition. Expects line/bar charts, week-over-week views, progress arcs. May accept denser layouts to show time dimension.
facade.rejectImplies: User prioritizes immediate insight, current status, simplicity. Wants big numbers, gauges, recent snapshot only. Wants to glance and know, not study and discover.

facade.hypothesis: The user prefers data visualization that feels understated and uses negative space rather than dense, colorful graphs
facade.label: Minimal line charts
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward calm, elegant presentation of data—favors simplicity and breathing room over visual richness
facade.rejectImplies: User wants more visual engagement, color coding, or density in charts—may prefer dashboard feel that's busier and more immediately information-dense

facade.hypothesis: Users want an active, encouraging presence—someone checking in on progress—rather than passive data display
facade.axisTargeted: Voice personality: passive data reporter vs. active encouragement coach
facade.label: Coach voice
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should include check-in messages, progress celebrations, and proactive nudges that feel like a supportive friend tracking experiments alongside them
facade.rejectImplies: Dashboard should present data neutrally without motivational language or personality-driven interactions; users prefer to interpret their own results independently
